facts.text: Essex Historical Society was founded in 1955 by citizens who wanted to preserve Hills Academy, the town’s first secondary school.
The building that now houses the library and offices of Essex Historical Society was bought from the town for just one dollar.
Essex Village sits on the eastern edge of the town, right along the banks of the Connecticut River.
All town government offices are located inside Essex Village, making it the administrative heart of the municipality.
Essex Historical Society curates more than 7,000 artifacts that chronicle the histories of Centerbrook, Essex, and Ivoryton.
The society runs a year‑round schedule of events that spotlight local history for residents and visitors alike.
